QF.id: Retirement Planning Products: National Pension System (NPS)_36

question.text: Under the Government Model, what happens to the NPS corpus on death of the subscriber?

answer.text: Entire corpus as lump sum to nominee
80% must be used for annuity and 20% as lump sum to family

explanation.text: **The Logic:** Under the Government Model, on death of the subscriber, 80% of the corpus must be used to purchase an annuity and 20% is paid as lump sum to the family. If corpus is Rs.5 lakh or less, entire amount is paid as lump sum. **The Trap:** This is different from the All Citizens Model where the entire corpus goes as lump sum. Govt Model has mandatory annuity even on death. **Cross-Exam Context:** The Govt Model death benefit ensures the family receives ongoing pension income (annuity) rather than a one-time payout. **Distributor's Pro-Tip:** Explain to government employee clients that their family will receive regular pension income through the annuity component on their death.
